name: Nagging
summary: Persistent interruptions requesting the user to perform a secondary action the user has already declined.

evidence.claim: Nagging is listed in Brignull's canonical deceptive-patterns taxonomy under Obstruction.

body.description: Repeated modals, push notifications, or in-page prompts asking the user to subscribe, enable notifications, install the app, or upgrade — after the user has already dismissed one or more (E1).

detectionHeuristics.description: Track modal impressions per session; flag when same modal-id fires after a dismiss event.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: Given a session's modal history (modal_id, dismissed_at timestamps), identify nagging patterns: same modal_id firing after an explicit dismiss. Return: {naggingModals: [{modalId, timesFired, timesDismissed}]}.
body.remediation: Offer the user a one-click 'dismiss all nags from this site for 30 days' action (extension feature, v0.2).
